events.event: Islamic Republic regime change by December 31, 2026
events.resolution_criteria: YES if non-Islamic Republic government recognized by 3+ UNSC members OR IRGC fragments into competing territorial factions. NO if Supreme Leader position filled and IRGC maintains territorial control of Tehran, Isfahan, and Tabriz.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: Strait of Hormuz reopens to normal commercial traffic by June 30, 2026
events.resolution_criteria: YES if Lloyd's reinstates standard marine insurance for Persian Gulf transit at pre-conflict premium levels. NO if war-risk premiums remain above 5x pre-conflict baseline.

history.note: Initial assessment

Updated from 25% to 55% (+30pp). Issue #3 analysis assesses 65% chance disruption resolves before mid-April. Lloyd's insurance normalization lags shipping resumption by weeks, but 25% was inconsistent with the disruption-duration estimate. 55% reflects insurance lag discount on the 65% shipping-resolution probability plus additional April-June resolution window.

Updated from 55% to 40% (-15pp). Duration model: 25-30% still disrupted at 120 days. But resolution criteria is Lloyd's insurance normalization, which lags physical reopening by weeks. June 30 timeline tight.

events.event: Ceasefire between US/Israel and Iran by April 30, 2026
events.resolution_criteria: YES if hostilities cease for 72+ consecutive hours under a stated agreement. NO if active strikes continue past April 30.

history.note: Initial assessment
Updated from 15% to 8% (-7pp). Larijani killed (potential negotiating counterpart removed), ceasefire rejected twice by Iran's supreme leader, new leadership vows continuation.

events.event: Iran tests or deploys a nuclear weapon by December 31, 2026
events.resolution_criteria: IAEA confirmation of nuclear test, seismic detection by CTBTO consistent with nuclear detonation, or credible first-use in conflict.

history.note: Initial assessment
Updated from 8% to 12% (+4pp). Bushehr nuclear facility shelled (Telegram OSINT). Under existential military pressure, breakout rationale strengthens.

events.event: ICE removal levels remain within 20% of Noem-era claimed figures through Q3 2026
events.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if ICE operational metrics remain within 20% of Noem-era claimed levels through Q3 2026. Resolves NO if metrics fall outside that range or if independent verification shows Noem-era claims were inflated by more than 20%.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: Mullin confirmed as DHS Secretary by April 15, 2026
events.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if Senate confirmation vote occurs and Mullin receives majority by April 15, 2026. Resolves NO if vote fails, is not held by April 15, or nomination is withdrawn.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: Federal charges related to DHS advertising contracts by December 2026
events.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if DOJ or Inspector General announces criminal charges against any individual related to DHS advertising or communications contracts awarded during Noem's tenure by December 31, 2026. Resolves NO if no charges announced by that date.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: DHS advertising spending drops >50% Q2 vs Q1 2026
events.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if USAspending data shows DHS advertising and communications category spending in Q2 2026 is less than 50% of Q1 2026 spending. Resolves NO if spending remains at or above 50% of Q1 levels.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: ICE removals exceed 700,000 in calendar year 2026
events.resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if independently verified ICE removal statistics show 700,000 or more removals in calendar year 2026. Resolves NO if verified count falls below 700,000. If no independent verification mechanism exists by December 31, 2026, defaults to NO.

history.note: Initial assessment

events.event: EU gas storage fails 80% mandate by October 2026
events.resolution_criteria: AGSI EU-wide aggregate storage below 80% on October 31, 2026.

history.note: Initial assessment. AGSI shows NL at 9%, DE at 21.5% entering refill season. Qatari LNG (12-14% of EU imports) offline. Germany recovering via Norwegian pipeline; Netherlands has no viable alternative.
Updated from 45% to 55% (+10pp). AGSI at 22% entering refill season. Hormuz disrupting Qatari LNG flows. If closure persists through summer, EU refill severely impacted.

events.event: Hormuz disruption persists more than 6 weeks
events.resolution_criteria: Commercial shipping through Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted (no LNG/oil tanker transit) as of April 13, 2026.

history.note: Initial assessment. Historical base rate of sustained full closure effectively zero. Current conflict unprecedented in scope (direct US-Iran engagement). Adjusted from near-zero to 35% for unprecedented scope while respecting base rate discipline.
Updated from 35% to 80% (+45pp). Liara duration model: 90% at 30 days. Day 15+ of active closure, no de-escalation pathway maturing. IRGC hardening, Larijani killed, ceasefire rejected twice.

events.event: Fertilizer scarcity reduces Northern Hemisphere yields more than 2% (conditional on closure through April)
events.resolution_criteria: USDA or FAO reporting yield reduction exceeding 2% on staple crops (wheat, corn, soybeans) in Northern Hemisphere harvest 2026, attributed to fertilizer under-application.

history.note: Initial assessment. CONDITIONAL on Hormuz closed through April. Gulf handles 20-30% of global fertilizer trade. Spring planting window is March-April. No structured fertilizer pricing data -- rests on web research.

events.event: Food prices spike more than 10% in H2 2026
events.resolution_criteria: FAO Food Price Index or relevant CPI food component increases more than 10% from June 2026 baseline by December 2026.

history.note: Initial assessment. Unconditional. Chain decomposition: P(closure) 0.35 x P(yield|closure) 0.60 x P(transmission|yield) 0.70 = 14.7%, rounded to 25% for model uncertainty and partial effects from shorter disruptions.
Updated from 25% to 35% (+10pp). Updated chain: P(closure through April) now 80% x P(yield|closure) 60% x P(transmission) 70% = 33.6%, rounded for model uncertainty.

events.event: US enters technical recession (2 consecutive negative real GDP quarters) in 2026
events.resolution_criteria: BEA reports two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth with onset in 2026. Resolution delayed to Q1 2027 to allow for Q4 2026 advance estimate.

history.note: Initial assessment. Compound shock model: oil shock (Hormuz, +41%) + trade disruption (17% tariff) + constrained Fed (3.64%, 3.1% core PCE). Historical 100% recession rate when all 3 vectors present (n=2: 1973-75, 1979-82). Tempered by lower inflation (3% vs 12-15%), stronger consumer balance sheets (11.3% debt service), ISM still expanding (52.4). Key swing: Hormuz duration.

events.event: US experiences growth recession (real GDP below 1.0% annualized for 2+ consecutive quarters) in 2026
events.resolution_criteria: BEA reports two or more consecutive quarters of real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized with onset in 2026.

history.note: Initial assessment. More likely than technical recession because consumer balance sheets (11.3% debt service) provide floor against full contraction. Compound shock drags below trend but structural resilience prevents two negative quarters. GDP 0-1% is the modal outcome.

resolved.resolution_note: Brent crossed $106 on approximately March 14 following Hormuz closure. Resolved YES.
